message:
test(fcp-conformance): pin nbf/exp boundary semantics for capability tokens (br-wsbgg)

auth_claims_conformance.rs uses far-future exp + far-past nbf, so the
at-boundary timing semantics that validate_timing_with_clock_skew
enforces (capability.rs:1762) were never exercised at the conformance
layer.

New tests/capability_token_timing_conformance.rs locks in 9 scenarios
across the nbf and exp bounds of the CAPABILITY_TOKEN_CLOCK_SKEW_SECS
(300s) grace window:

  nbf bounds:
    - nbf == now: verifies (issuance moment is valid)
    - nbf in future within skew: verifies (operator clock-drift grace)
    - nbf in future beyond skew: rejected with TokenNotYetValid

  exp bounds:
    - exp == now: verifies within skew (FCP deviation from RFC 7519
      §4.1.4 strict comparison; intentional)
    - exp recently past within skew: verifies
    - exp past beyond skew: rejected with TokenExpired

  pathological windows:
    - zero-duration window inside skew: verifies (skew-aware bounds
      are independent; no degenerate-window short-circuit)
    - inverted nbf>exp with exp beyond skew: TokenExpired (exp check
      runs first)
    - inverted bounds beyond skew on both sides: TokenExpired (exp
      check still surfaces first)

Tests use a 60-second buffer past the skew window for negative cases
to absorb scheduling jitter between issuance and verification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
